ChassisPowerMonitor.title: Chassis Input Power Monitoring Definition
ChassisPowerMonitor.description: A schema detailing the hardware used to monitor input power for a chassis. Currently implemented with gpios.

PresenceGpio.description: The GPIO to monitor that indicates whether this chassis is present.

FaultUnlatchedGpio.description: The GPIO to monitor that indicates the current (live) state of the input power of this chassis.

FaultLatchedGpio.description: The GPIO to monitor that indicates if an input power fault was latched for this chassis (eg between reads of unlatched gpio).

FaultLatchResetGpio.description: The GPIO to disable (reset) the latched input power loss gpio for this chassis.

EnableSystemResetGpio.description: The GPIO to enable a full system reset if/when this chassis has a loss of input power.

FSILinkCheck.description: The device path to check existence of to verify the fsi link to this chassis is working. Eg UCD i2c bus.

ChassisID.description: The ID of the chassis these GPIOs are for.
